lines.en_lit: the tops of the stems and sprinkle it. Abaye said: Even you say Rabbi Akiva, performs the service the entire day and toward the evening sprinkle upon him. immerses and observes until sunset,

burns the incense and removes the lamps. Apparently, incense first, and then the lamps And raises a contradiction who was privileged removal of the ashes from the inner altar, the one who was privileged the candelabrum, the one who is privileged the incense.

Rav Huna said: Who is taught Tamid? It is Rabbi Shimon Ish HaMitzpa, But didn’t we learn the opposite, as we learned He comes to the northeast corner sprinkles northeast southwest corner sprinkles southwest
And it was taught concerning Rabbi Shimon Ish HaMitzpa changes the daily offering northeast sprinkles northeast southwest he sprinkles west and then sprinkles south
Rather, Rabbi Yoḥanan said: Who taught tractate Yoma? It is Rabbi Shimon Ish HaMitzpa,

raises a contradiction tractate Yoma against tractate Yoma, as we learned The second lottery Who slaughters who sprinkles who removes the ashes the inner altar; and who removes the ashes the candelabrum; and who takes the limbs up to the ramp the third lottery. new to the incense participate in the lottery
Abaye said: not difficult. Here, the removal two lamps, there, the removal five lamps,

Is that to say that the incense interposes But Abaye related the order of the priestly functions in the name of tradition, didn’t the blood of the daily offering that interposed between say not difficult. That Abba Shaul, this the Rabbis, as it was taught One not remove the lamps and then burn rather one should burn and then remove Abba Shaul says: One removes and then burns
What is the rationale Abba Shaul? as it is written: “Every morning when he removes the ashes from the lamps, and then “He shall burn it” And the Rabbis, What is the Merciful One saying?
